name: Byzantine Empire
continuity_note: Explicit project convention: new entity and full Elo reset at 395. The predecessor link is descriptive and transfers no rating.

name: First Bulgarian Empire
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Venice
continuity_note: The Most Serene Republic from the traditional first doge (697, a convention) to Napoleon's abolition (12 May 1797). Retained as a single continuous institution per the long-envelope audit convention.

name: Cornwall
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Aghlabid Dynasty
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Scotland
continuity_note: Conventional founding under Kenneth MacAlpin (843) to the Acts of Union (1707). The 843 start is a convention; ninth-century precision is not implied.

name: East Franks
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: West Franks
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Danelaw

name: Fatimid Caliphate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of León
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of England
continuity_note: From AEthelstan's unification (927) to the Acts of Union (1 May 1707). The Commonwealth and Protectorate (1649-1660) are conventionally catalogued as a distinct republic, so - mirroring the French First Republic reset - label and code windows gap 1649-1660 and Interregnum rows stay staged pending a curated Commonwealth identity. The United Kingdom identity begins at 1707 and inherits no rating; labels naming England after 1706 stay staged.

name: Caliphate of Córdoba
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Hamdanid Emirates
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of France
continuity_note: Ends before revolutionary and Napoleonic entities, which receive independent ratings.

name: County of Flanders
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Hungary
continuity_note: From Stephen I's coronation (1000/1001) to the defeat at Mohacs (29 August 1526) and the kingdom's trisection. Habsburg Royal Hungary thereafter fights under the Habsburg identity; Ottoman Hungary under the Ottoman identity.

name: Taifa of Badajoz
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Great Seljuk Empire
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Portugal
region: Southwestern Europe and Portuguese overseas possessions
continuity_note: Curated kingdom beginning with the 1143 Treaty of Zamora recognition and ending before Brazil's 1815 elevation created the United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves. The later union and restored kingdom inherit no rating.

name: Ayyubid Sultanate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Mongol Empire
continuity_note: Unified imperial line only; khanates require separate future records.

name: Hafsid Dynasty
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Teutonic Order
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Mamluk Sultanate
continuity_note: Cairo-based Mamluk Sultanate through Ottoman conquest.

name: Golden Horde
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Ottoman Empire
region: Southeastern Europe, West Asia, and North Africa
continuity_note: The Republic of Turkey would be a new entity and does not inherit this rating.

name: Principality of Wallachia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Principality of Moldavia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Northern Yuan
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Timurid Empire
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Joseon
continuity_note: The Yi dynasty kingdom from 1392 to the proclamation of the Korean Empire (October 1897). The Korean Empire (1897-1910) and the modern Korean states stay staged under this label.

name: Aztec Triple Alliance
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Inca Empire
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Crimean Khanate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Haudenosaunee
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Warring States Japan
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Spanish Monarchy and Empire
continuity_note: Aggregated imperial Spanish polity for demonstration; future production data should adjudicate regime boundaries more finely.

name: Bijapur Sultanate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Safavid Iran
continuity_note: Distinct Safavid polity; later Iranian dynasties require separate ratings.

name: Kingdom of Spain
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Denmark
continuity_note: The Danish kingdom from the effective end of the Kalmar Union (1523). The personal union with Norway (to 1814) is not a separate rating identity; medieval Denmark stays staged.

name: Kingdom of Sweden
continuity_note: The modern Swedish state from Gustav Vasa's election (6 June 1523) after the breakup of the Kalmar Union. Historiography treats later constitutional changes (1611, 1721, 1809) as the same continuing kingdom. Medieval and Kalmar-era Sweden stays staged; this identity replaces the pending-split quarantine of the bare label.

name: Habsburg Monarchy
continuity_note: The composite Danubian monarchy dated from Ferdinand I's election to the Bohemian and Hungarian crowns after Mohacs (1526) to the proclamation of the Empire of Austria (11 August 1804). Until Charles V's abdications (1556) the dynasty's Danubian and Spanish-Imperial branches shared the 'Habsburg' label in the sources, so label and code windows begin 1556 and 1526-1555 rows stay staged; the identity record starts 1526 because the Danubian monarchy itself dates from Ferdinand's election. Pre-1526 Habsburg hereditary lands stay staged.

name: Mughal Empire
continuity_note: Conventional Mughal imperial span; periods of reduced control remain part of the same demonstration entity.

name: Tsardom of Russia
continuity_note: Existing time-bounded Cliopatria identity, curated from Ivan IV's coronation as the first Russian tsar in 1547 through the last complete pre-imperial year in 1720. The Russian Empire begins a new rating in 1721 and inherits nothing.

name: Dutch Revolt and Republic
continuity_note: Begins with the revolt for this demonstration so its independence war can be rated; the later Kingdom of the Netherlands is separate.

name: Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth
continuity_note: From the Union of Lublin (1 July 1569) to the Third Partition (October 1795). The pre-1569 Jagiellonian personal union and the medieval Kingdom of Poland stay staged.

name: Kingdom of Mysore
continuity_note: Independent Mysore from the post-Vijayanagara consolidation (Cliopatria's 1572 interval) to the fall of Seringapatam (4 May 1799). The Hyder Ali/Tipu Sultan takeover is a dynastic change within the same polity, not a reset. The post-1799 princely state stays staged.

name: Dzungar Khanate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Bhutan
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Parliamentarian Forces (Wars of the Three Kingdoms)
continuity_note: Curated civil-war belligerent 1642-1651 (Edgehill to Worcester). From May 1649 the same army served the Commonwealth state; the 1649-1651 tail is kept inside this faction identity as an explicit contested-boundary decision (conventional single-belligerent treatment of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms), flagged for second review. A future Commonwealth state identity supersedes those rows only through review; no rating transfers in either direction.

name: Royalist Forces (Wars of the Three Kingdoms)
continuity_note: Curated civil-war belligerent: the field armies raised for the King from the royal standard at Nottingham (August 1642) to Worcester (September 1651), including the 1650-1651 Scottish-raised army of Charles II (a contested-boundary inclusion following the conventional 'English Civil Wars 1642-51' envelope; flagged for second review). The restored monarchy of 1660 is a continuation of the English crown, not of this faction identity; no rating is inherited in either direction.

name: Qing Dynasty
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Morocco
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Maratha Confederacy
continuity_note: From Shivaji's coronation at Raigad (6 June 1674) to the abolition of the Peshwa after the Third Anglo-Maratha War (June 1818). Post-1818 princely successor states (Gwalior, Indore, Baroda) stay staged. Supersedes the Cliopatria 'Maratha Empire' 1677-1845 interval as the rating identity.

name: Kingdom of Prussia
continuity_note: Coronation of Frederick I on 18 January 1701 to the proclamation of the German Empire on 18 January 1871, after which Prussian forces fight under the imperial identity. Brandenburg-Prussia stays staged.

name: Ashanti Empire
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: United Kingdom
region: Western Europe and former overseas empire
continuity_note: The sovereign United Kingdom is the rated entity; imperial status is an era tag rather than a successor ID here.

name: Russian Empire
continuity_note: Ends in 1917; the Soviet Union is independently initialized.

name: Afsharid Iran
continuity_note: Nader Shah's coronation (March 1736) to his assassination (June 1747), after which the empire fragments. The label window starts 1737 so 1736 events keep the committed Safavid attribution. The Zand era stays staged.

name: Sultanate of Oman
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Durrani Empire
continuity_note: Ahmad Shah Durrani's election at Kandahar (1747) to the deposition of the last Sadozai ruler of Kabul (1823). The Hotak era (1709-1738) stays staged.

name: Kuwait
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Nepal
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: United States
continuity_note: Continuous rated polity from the declaration-era independence struggle for this demonstration.

name: French First Republic
continuity_note: Proclaimed 21-22 September 1792 after the fall of the monarchy; ends with the proclamation of the First Empire on 18 May 1804. New identity at baseline; no rating crosses 1792 or 1804. Label windows use 1793-1803 so boundary-year events stay with the adjacent convention already committed.

name: Qajar Iran
continuity_note: From Agha Mohammad Khan's defeat of the last Zand ruler at Kerman (1794, the Britannica dating; his unification campaign began 1789 and he was crowned 1796) to the deposition of Ahmad Shah and the Pahlavi accession (December 1925). Supersedes the Cliopatria 'Qajar Dynasty' 1788-1923 interval as the rating identity.

name: Sikh Empire
continuity_note: Ranjit Singh's capture of Lahore (July 1799) to the British annexation of the Punjab after the Second Anglo-Sikh War (29 March 1849). The pre-1799 misl confederacy stays staged.

name: Austrian Empire
continuity_note: Proclaimed 11 August 1804; transformed by the Austro-Hungarian Compromise of March-June 1867. New identity at baseline; no rating crosses 1804 or 1867.

name: First French Empire
continuity_note: Explicit new imperial entity; predecessor link is lineage metadata and transfers no Elo.

name: Egypt (Muhammad Ali dynasty)
aliases: Egypt of the Muhammad Ali dynasty
continuity_note: Muhammad Ali's investiture as wali (1805) to the British occupation (September 1882); autonomous Egyptian command under nominal Ottoman suzerainty (vassal rule: rated separately because it fielded forces under materially autonomous command, including against the Ottomans). The formal Khedivate title dates from 1867; the record uses the conventional dynasty-era window. Post-1882 Egypt under British occupation stays staged.

name: Sokoto Caliphate
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Duchy of Warsaw
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: (Netherlands)
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Paraguay
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Chile
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Norway
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Bourbon Restoration France
continuity_note: The Second Bourbon Restoration is rated as a separate polity from 1815 through the 1830 regime change. It is available only to exact reviewed source contracts; bare France labels remain unresolved in this interval and no rating transfers from the First Empire or to the July Monarchy.

name: United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves
region: Portugal, Brazil, and Portuguese overseas possessions
continuity_note: Separate pluricontinental sovereign polity created by the 16 December 1815 elevation of Brazil and ending with Brazilian independence in 1822. Generic year-only policy begins in 1816 and ends in 1821, leaving both transition years unresolved; no rating transfers across either boundary.

name: Burma
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Greek Revolutionary Forces (War of Independence)
continuity_note: Revolutionary belligerent from the outbreak of March 1821 through the provisional administrations (Epidaurus 1822) and the Kapodistrian Hellenic State, ending with the establishment of the Kingdom of Greece (1832). Includes Ypsilantis's 1821 Danubian campaign under the conventional single-war treatment (declared criterion-1 exception, flagged for second review). The Kingdom is a separate future identity; no rating transfers.

name: Empire of Brazil
continuity_note: Independence proclaimed 7 September 1822; the republic proclaimed 15 November 1889. Supersedes the identically-windowed Cliopatria 'Empire of Brazil' interval as the rating identity; the republic eras stay on their Cliopatria intervals.

name: Gran Colombia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Portugal (restored)
region: Southwestern Europe and Portuguese overseas possessions
continuity_note: Separate post-union Portuguese kingdom beginning with Brazil's 1822 separation and ending with the republican revolution of 5 October 1910. Generic year-only policy runs from 1823 through 1909, leaving both transition years unresolved; independently dated 1822 actions require exact candidate contracts, and no rating is inherited from the pre-1815 kingdom or the union.

name: Republic of Peru
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Emirate of Afghanistan
continuity_note: Barakzai rule from the fall of the Sadozais (1823) to Amanullah's proclamation of the Kingdom (1926). Covers all three Anglo-Afghan wars. Until Dost Mohammad's annexations (Kandahar 1855, Herat June 1863) Afghanistan was fragmented among Kabul, Kandahar, and the Sadozai principality of Herat; records naming those rivals as belligerents are curated exclusions, not this identity. The 1926-1973 Kingdom and the post-1979 regimes (already policy-gated) stay staged.

name: Mexican Republic
continuity_note: From the 1824 federal constitution (after the First Empire) to the French installation of the Second Empire (regency proclaimed 1863, Maximilian crowned 1864). Federalist/centralist constitutional swings are the same polity by convention. The 1864-1867 Second Empire and the restored republic stay on their Cliopatria intervals.

name: Bolivia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Belgium
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Argentine Confederation
continuity_note: From the Federal Pact (January 1831) to the Battle of Pavon and national reunification under Buenos Aires (1861). The 1810-1831 United Provinces era and the post-1862 unified republic stay staged (the republic's Cliopatria interval begins 1930).

name: Kingdom of Greece
continuity_note: From the Treaty of Constantinople (July 1832) establishing the independent kingdom to the proclamation of the Second Hellenic Republic (25 March 1924). The independence-war provisional governments and the interwar republic stay staged.

name: Carlist Forces (First Carlist War)
continuity_note: The pretender Don Carlos's army from the 1833 rising to the Convention of Vergara and the fall of Morella (1839-1840). The later Carlist risings of 1846-1849 and 1872-1876 are separate movements requiring their own identities; no rating transfers.

name: Republic of Ecuador
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Venezuela
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Peru-Bolivian Confederation
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Guatemala
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Honduras
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of El Salvador
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Nicaragua
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Taiping Heavenly Kingdom
continuity_note: Rebel proto-state from the Jintian uprising and proclamation of the Heavenly Kingdom (January 1851) to the fall of Tianjing (July 1864). Remnant forces after 1864 are outside the identity. No successor inherits its rating.

name: Second French Empire
continuity_note: Proclaimed 2 December 1852; ends with the capitulation at Sedan and the proclamation of the Third Republic on 4 September 1870. New identity at baseline; the Second Republic (1848-1852) has no curated identity and stays a deliberate gap.

name: South African Republic
continuity_note: Recognized at the Sand River Convention (January 1852); extinguished at the Treaty of Vereeniging (31 May 1902). The 1877-1881 British annexation is treated as an interruption of the same polity per convention, not a reset.

name: Montenegro
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Ethiopian Empire
continuity_note: The modern Ethiopian Empire from Tewodros II's unification ending the Zemene Mesafint (February 1855) to the Italian occupation (May 1936). The medieval and Gondarine Solomonic eras stay staged pending their own identities; the 1941 restoration and the modern state stay on the 1942-2024 Cliopatria interval.

name: Free Orange State
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Mexican Conservative Forces
continuity_note: Conservative side of the War of the Reform 1857-1861. Conservative collaboration with the French Intervention and the Second Mexican Empire (1862-1867) is a different era and belligerent structure, deliberately outside this identity.

name: Mexican Liberal (Constitutionalist) Forces
continuity_note: Constitutionalist side of the War of the Reform, from the Plan of Tacubaya coup (December 1857) to the liberal entry into Mexico City (January 1861). The restored constitutional government of Mexico is a state identity outside this faction window; no rating transfers.

name: Confederate States of America
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: United States of Colombia
continuity_note: Curated identity beginning with the 1863 federal constitution and ending before the Republic of Colombia identity took effect in 1886. Generic 'Colombia' is deliberately not an alias, and no rating crosses the constitutional reset.

name: Austria-Hungary
continuity_note: The Dual Monarchy from the 1867 Compromise to its dissolution in October-November 1918. Successor republics receive no inherited rating.

name: Cuban Insurgent Army (Ten Years' War)
continuity_note: Mambi insurgency from the Grito de Yara (October 1868) to the Pact of Zanjon (February 1878). The 1895 Liberation Army is a separate later identity; namesake continuity is forbidden.

name: Empire of Japan
continuity_note: Imperial constitutional and expansionist-era entity; postwar Japan requires a new record for future conflicts.

name: Mexico
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: French Third Republic
continuity_note: Proclaimed 4 September 1870; ends with the vote of full powers to Petain on 10 July 1940. Events dated only '1870' are ambiguous between Empire and Republic and deliberately stay staged. Vichy France and Free France (1940-1944) have no curated identity.

name: German Empire
continuity_note: Ends with the imperial regime in 1918; Nazi Germany is a later, independently rated entity.

name: Kingdom of Romania
continuity_note: Proclaimed a kingdom (March 1881, independent since 1878) to the forced abdication of Michael I (30 December 1947). The pre-1881 United Principalities and the People's Republic stay staged.

name: Kingdom of Serbia
continuity_note: Proclaimed a kingdom (March 1882, independent since the 1878 Treaty of Berlin) until absorption into the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (1 December 1918). COW/IWD label this polity 'Yugoslavia' under code 345; the code policy maps 1882-1918 records here and leaves interwar and later Yugoslavia staged.

name: Cuban Liberation Army (War of Independence)
continuity_note: Liberation Army from the Grito de Baire (February 1895) to the armistice of August 1898. The Republic of Cuba (1902) is a separate polity; no rating transfers.

name: Commonwealth of Australia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Cuba
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Panama
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: First Portuguese Republic
region: Southwestern Europe and Portuguese overseas possessions
continuity_note: Republic proclaimed on 5 October 1910 and ended by the military coup of 28 May 1926. Year-only source policies use the unambiguous complete years 1911-1925; neither boundary year is assigned, and the republic inherits no rating from the monarchy.

name: Albania
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of China
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Armenia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: First Republic of Estonia
continuity_note: Separate first-republic rating identity from the February 1918 proclamation to the 1940 Soviet occupation. The restored republic receives no inherited rating, and this identity is attached only by exact reviewed contracts.

name: Mutawakkilite Kingdom of Yemen
continuity_note: Curated post-Ottoman kingdom beginning in 1918. The policy stops at the last complete pre-coup year, 1961, so the 1962 transition is not assigned by a year-only source. Generic 'Yemen' and the broader North Yemen envelope are deliberately excluded.

name: Republic of Finland
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Lithuania
continuity_note: Independence declared 16 February 1918; Soviet annexation June-August 1940. The medieval Grand Duchy and the post-1990 restored republic stay staged; no rating crosses 1940.

name: Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic
continuity_note: Curated revolutionary Russian actor from the 1918 RSFSR constitutional settlement through the 1922 formation of the USSR. It is available only to exact reviewed IWD/IWBD contracts; the generic Russia 1918-1921 resolver gap remains closed and no rating transfers to the Soviet Union.

name: Second Polish Republic
continuity_note: Independence declared November 1918; destroyed by the German-Soviet invasion of September-October 1939. The government-in-exile and People's Republic receive no inherited rating.

name: Czechoslovakia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Hungarian Republic
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Turkish National Movement
continuity_note: Autonomous national movement and Ankara government rated for the 1919-1922 military struggle, distinct from the contemporaneous Ottoman government and the later Republic of Turkey. Only exact reviewed contracts may bind bare Turkey to this identity; the general 1919-1923 deny window remains in force.

name: Yugoslavia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Soviet Union
continuity_note: New polity and baseline reset; no Russian Empire Elo is inherited.

name: Republic of Turkey
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Chinese Communist Forces
continuity_note: CCP-commanded forces (Red Army, later People's Liberation Army) from the Nanchang uprising (1 August 1927) to the proclamation of the People's Republic (1949). The People's Republic of China is a separate curated polity and inherits no rating from this actor.

name: Argentine Republic
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Second Spanish Republic
continuity_note: Proclaimed 14 April 1931; ends with the surrender of Madrid and the end of the Civil War (1 April 1939). Francoist Spain is a distinct successor regime and inherits no rating. State identity created inside the actor tranche under the incumbent-side carve-out (reopens no declared gap; sole opposing side of a curated actor; attested conventional boundaries).

name: Canada
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Kingdom of Iraq
continuity_note: Independent from the end of the British mandate (3 October 1932) to the 14 July 1958 revolution. The republic eras stay on their Cliopatria intervals; no rating crosses 1958.

name: Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
continuity_note: Curated identity beginning with the proclamation effective 23 September 1932. Generic 'Saudi Arabia' is deliberately not an alias; COW 670 reaches this identity only through its exact time-bounded code policy.

name: South Africa
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Nazi Germany
continuity_note: Treated as a distinct historiographic regime-state. It ends in terminal military defeat, occupation, and regime destruction in 1945.

name: Spanish Nationalist Faction (1936-1939)
continuity_note: Insurgent civil-war belligerent from the July 1936 rising to victory on 1 April 1939. Its succession into Francoist Spain (a registry identity) is an explicit continuity decision that transfers no rating.

name: Ethiopia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Lebanon
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Democratic Republic of Vietnam
continuity_note: Distinct wartime state; unified post-1976 Vietnam would be a successor entity in expanded data.

continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: State of Israel
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: People's Republic of China
continuity_note: Begins as a new polity in 1949; predecessor civil-war actors are outside this seed.

name: Republic of Egypt
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: 26th of July Movement
continuity_note: Organized as a movement in 1955; fielded force from the Granma landing (December 1956) to victory in January 1959. The 1953 Moncada attack predates the movement's organized existence and stays staged. The post-1959 Cuban state is a separate identity; no rating transfers.

name: Republic of Vietnam
continuity_note: Ends in existential defeat and state dissolution in 1975.

name: Kingdom of Cambodia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: Republic of Tunisia
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: French Fifth Republic
continuity_note: The 1958 constitutional founding starts a new rated identity at the baseline. Earlier French kingdoms, empires, and republics are separate entities, and none of their Elo is inherited.

name: Communist Cuba
continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

continuity_note: Time-bounded Cliopatria interval. Namesakes, predecessors, and successors receive no inherited rating without an explicit continuity decision.

name: National Liberation Front of South Vietnam
continuity_note: Southern insurgent front founded 20 December 1960, dissolved into the unified Vietnamese state in 1976. Operationally aligned with the DRV but rated separately as a materially distinct fielded force; neither the DRV nor unified Vietnam inherits its rating. HCED rows labelling PAVN engagements as 'Viet Cong' are held on the curated exclusion list, not absorbed.

name: Taliban
continuity_note: Included because state-versus-insurgent wars cannot be rated accurately without the opposing armed actor; governmental episodes are not treated as inherited from the Afghan Republic.

name: Islamic Republic of Afghanistan
continuity_note: Rated only for its 2004-2021 existence; it ends in state collapse during the Taliban offensive.
